name: Protecting Your Faith
epithet: What to do when doubt whispers, and how to drive Shaytan away
title: Du'as Against Doubt and Shaytan: protecting your faith, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
meta_description: What the Prophet ﷺ taught for whispers of doubt about faith and for repelling Shaytan, in Arabic, transliteration and English. From the sunnah, via Buruja.
keywords: dua against waswas, whispers of doubt, doubt in faith, repel Shaytan, dua against shaitan, waswas of faith, doubts about Allah, seeking refuge from Satan, protecting iman, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: Sometimes the attack does not come from outside at all. It comes as a whisper: a thought about Allah you would never speak aloud, a question about your own belief that arrives unbidden and leaves you shaken. Many sincere believers carry this in silence, afraid that the whisper itself is proof their faith has failed.

It is not. The Prophet ﷺ was asked about exactly this, and his companions told him they would sooner fall from the sky than utter what crossed their minds. He answered that feeling the whisper and hating it is itself a sign of clear faith. Here is what he taught us to do when it comes.
intro: The guidance here is as much a practice as a recitation. When a doubt about Allah is thrown at you, the Prophet ﷺ taught two things: seek refuge in Allah, and stop. Do not entertain the thought, do not argue with it, do not chase it down. Turn away and affirm your belief, and the whisper loses its grip.

Alongside these specific responses, the everyday adhkar are your steady defence: the morning and evening remembrance, the verses before sleep, and reciting Surah al-Baqarah in the home, which the Prophet ﷺ said Shaytan flees. Whispers feed on an empty, idle heart; they retreat from one kept busy with the remembrance of Allah.
closing_heading: Do not be afraid of the whisper
closing: The most important thing to know is that being troubled by these thoughts is a mercy, not a verdict. A heart that did not care would not be disturbed. Your distress at the whisper is the very evidence of the faith you are afraid you have lost.

O Allah, make our hearts firm upon Your religion, guard us from the whispering of the retreating whisperer, and let our faith be the last thing taken from us. You are the One who holds the heavens from falling; hold our hearts steady upon belief in You.

faq.q: I keep getting evil or doubting thoughts about Allah and my faith. Have I become a disbeliever?
faq.a: No. The companions came to the Prophet ﷺ distressed by exactly these thoughts, saying they would rather fall from the sky than speak them. He told them that feeling this and hating it is 'clear faith.' The whisper is from Shaytan, not from you, and being disturbed by it is a sign your faith is alive, not gone.

faq.q: What should I actually do when a doubt about Allah or Islam is whispered to me?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ taught a clear response: seek refuge in Allah (say A'udhu billah) and then stop. Do not dwell on the thought or try to argue it out. Turn away from it, affirm your belief in Allah and His messengers, and busy yourself with something else. Refusing to engage is itself the cure.

faq.q: How can I protect myself from Shaytan in general?
faq.a: Keep your heart occupied with the remembrance of Allah. The Prophet ﷺ taught that Shaytan flees the home in which Surah al-Baqarah is recited, that the morning and evening adhkar drive him away, and that saying La ilaha illallah... a hundred times is a protection from him for the day. A heart full of dhikr leaves him no empty room to enter.

email.subject: When doubt whispers
email.alt_subject: The thought you would never say aloud
email.preview: What the Prophet ﷺ taught for whispers of doubt and for driving Shaytan away.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

Sometimes the attack does not come from outside at all. It comes as a whisper: a thought about Allah you would never speak aloud, a question about your own belief that arrives unbidden and leaves you shaken. Many sincere believers carry this in silence, afraid that the whisper itself is proof their faith has failed.

It is not. The companions came to the Prophet ﷺ troubled by exactly these thoughts, and he told them that feeling the whisper and hating it is itself 'clear faith.' A heart that did not care would not be disturbed. His guidance was simple: seek refuge in Allah, then stop. Do not argue with the thought or chase it down. Turn away, affirm your belief, and it loses its grip.

Alongside that, keep your heart occupied: the morning and evening adhkar, the verses before sleep, Surah al-Baqarah in the home that Shaytan flees. The whisper feeds on an idle heart and retreats from one busy with the remembrance of Allah.

The full guidance, with Arabic, transliteration and meaning, is here:

social.hook:
A thought about Allah you would never say aloud.

The Prophet ﷺ said being disturbed by it is a sign of clear faith, not lost faith.
social.caption:
When doubt whispers: what the Prophet ﷺ taught.

His companions came to him troubled by thoughts they would rather fall from the sky than speak. He did not scold them. He told them that hating the whisper is itself 'clear faith.'

The response he taught is simple: seek refuge in Allah, then stop. Do not argue with the thought or chase it down. Turn away, affirm your belief, and keep your heart busy with His remembrance, the adhkar, Surah al-Baqarah in the home that Shaytan flees.

If you carry this quietly, you are not alone, and you are not lost. Save this and read the full guidance with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/protection-iman

#Waswas #DoubtInFaith #ProtectingIman #Dhikr #Dua #HisnAlMuslim #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

source_note: Prophetic adhkar transcribed verbatim from Hisn al-Muslim (Sa'eed al-Qahtani) via sunnah.com; al-Bukhari and Muslim taken as sahih, jayyid/reliable as hasan. Recitation audio: the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com) for the adhkar. Selection follows Hisn al-Muslim, with thanks to lifewithallah.com; framing is Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: confirm every Arabic text, reference, repetition count and grading before publication.

items.title: Seek refuge, and stop
items.arabic: يَسْتَعِيذُ بِاللَّهِ. يَنْتَهِي عَمَّا شك فِيهِ
items.en: He should seek refuge in Allah and stop pursuing the thought.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said that when Shaytan throws a doubt at you, asking 'who created this, who created that' until he asks 'who created Allah', you should seek refuge in Allah and cease. The whisper dies the moment you refuse to follow it.

items.title: I believe in Allah and His messengers
items.en: I believe in Allah and His Messengers.
items.note: The other half of the response: instead of engaging the doubt, you answer it with a clear affirmation of faith. Belief stated plainly is the cure for belief questioned.

items.title: He is the First and the Last
items.arabic: يقرأ قوله تعالى (هُوَ الأوَّلُ، وَالآخِـرُ، وَالظّـاهِـرُ، وَالْبـاطِـنُ، وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَيءٍ عَلـيم)
items.translit: Huwa ‘l-Awwalu wa ‘l-Ākhir, waẓ-Ẓāhiru wal-Bāṭin, wa huwa bikulli shay'in `Alīm.
items.en: (Recite the Ayat) He is the First and the Last, the Most High and the Most Near. And He is the Knower of all things (in Arabic).
items.note: Reciting this verse (al-Hadid 57:3) is taught as a remedy when persistent whispers will not leave, anchoring the heart in Allah's eternity and nearness when the mind is being pulled in circles.

items.title: Seek refuge from the outcast Shaytan
items.en: Seeking refuge with Allah against him (i.e. by saying I seek refuge in Allah from Satan the outcast).
items.note: The plain weapon against him: A'udhu billahi min ash-shaytan ir-rajim, taught in the Qur'an itself as the response to his provocation.

items.title: The call to prayer
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said that when the adhan is called, Shaytan turns and flees. The very announcement of prayer drives him off.

items.title: Remembrance and reciting the Qur'an
items.en: Saying words of Allah's remembrance (Thikr) and recitation of the Qur'an.
items.note: Shaytan flees the home in which Surah al-Baqarah is recited, and saying La ilaha illallah... a hundred times is a protection from him for the day. A heart kept busy with dhikr leaves him no room.

items.title: Guarding against the Dajjal
items.arabic: وَالْاسْتِعَاذَةُ بِاللَّهِ مِنْ فِتْنَتِهِ عَقِبَ التَّشَهُّدِ الْأَخِيرِ مِنْ كُلِّ صَلاَةٍ
items.en: Whoever memorizes ten 'Ayat (Verses) from the beginning of Surat Al-Kahf, will be protected from the False Messiah if he recites in every prayer after the final Tashahhud before ending the prayer, seeking the protection of Allah from the trials of the False Messiah.
items.ref: Muslim 1/555, another version mentions the last ten 'Ayat, Muslim 1/556
items.note: Whoever memorises the first ten verses of Surah al-Kahf is protected from the trial of the Dajjal.

items.title: Refuge from hidden shirk
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ إِنّـي أَعـوذُبِكَ أَنْ أُشْـرِكَ بِكَ وَأَنا أَعْـلَمْ، وَأَسْتَـغْفِرُكَ لِما لا أَعْـلَم
items.translit: Allāhumma innī a`ūdhu bika an ushrika bika wa anā a`lam, wa astaghfiruka limā lā a`lam.
items.en: O Allah, I seek refuge in You lest I associate anything with You knowingly, and I seek Your forgiveness for what I know not.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ taught this against shirk so subtle he likened it to the creeping of an ant, unseen even by the one who falls into it.

items.title: When a bad omen unsettles you
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ لا طَيْـرَ إِلاّ طَيْـرُك، وَلا خَـيْرَ إِلاّ خَـيْرُك، وَلا إِلهَ غَيْـرُك
items.translit: Allāhumma lā ṭayra illā ṭayruk, wa lā khayra illā khayruk, wa lā ilāha ghayruk.
items.en: O Allah there is no portent other than Your portent, no goodness other than Your goodness, and none worthy of worship other than You.
items.note: There is no truth in omens. This du'a hands all good and all power back to Allah and walks on.
